element_introduction_order.element: Basic 5-button control scheme (2 punch, 2 kick, block)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns directional attacks (leg sweep, uppercut) against first opponent
Gradually discovered through experimentation; each character has unique special move library
Players learn blocking takes reduced damage and enables easier counterattacks

element_introduction_order.element: Juggling (knocking opponent airborne for combo follow-ups)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Uppercut initiates juggle state; advanced technique requiring timing

element_introduction_order.element: Test Your Might minigame (rapid button pressing to break materials)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive difficulty: wood → stone → steel → ruby → diamond; score-based rewards
Tests mastery of chosen character against identical skillset

element_introduction_order.element: Endurance matches (2v1, health meter does not regenerate between fights)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource management; no healing between consecutive fights; timer resets
Context-sensitive execution; varies per character; censored in SNES version
High-difficulty opponent with advanced AI; undefeated champion in lore for 500 years
Final challenge; tournament culmination; controls fate of Earthrealm

typical_relief_triggers: Winning a round (health bar depletes opponent)
Test Your Might minigame breaks (score-based relief)
Completing a match (transition to next opponent)
typical_tension_triggers: Health bar depletion (rounds timed; critical health states)
Endurance match structure (consecutive fights without health regeneration)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty curve with breathing room between matches; Test Your Might minigames provide score-focused relief; late-game endurance matches and boss encounters create sustained tension with no health recovery

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Alternate Fatality exists as hidden content (requires PAR code; Johnny Cage uppercut variant cut from final release)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Player faces 7 standard matchups against playable characters; introduces core mechanics (blocking, special moves, juggling)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Test Your Might minigames interspersed for score rewards; Mirror Match tests character mastery; increased AI aggression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Endurance matches (2v1 with no health regeneration between fights) create resource scarcity; Goro sub-boss represents significant skill gate; Shang Tsung final match combines all mechanics
notable_spikes: First endurance match transition (2v1 structure, no healing)
Goro encounter (500-year undefeated champion; noted as sub-boss difficulty spike)
Shang Tsung final match (ultimate tournament stakes)

notable_level_patterns.description: 1v1 fight on arena stage; defeat opponent to advance; single health pool per match; optional Fatality execution on defeated opponent
Player faces duplicate of their chosen character; tests mastery of selected fighter's moveset and strategies
notable_level_patterns.example_level: After 7 standard matches, before endurance sequence

notable_level_patterns.description: 3 consecutive matches; each spawns 2 opponents sequentially; timer resets between opponents but player health does not regenerate; forces resource management
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Post-Mirror Match; three separate endurance encounters

notable_level_patterns.description: Sub-boss (Goro) followed immediately by final boss (Shang Tsung); culminates tournament narrative; highest difficulty encounters
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Final two matches of single-player campaign

notable_level_patterns.description: Rapid button-press meter-filling challenge to break materials; progressive difficulty (wood → stone → steel → ruby → diamond); score-based rewards; appears between certain campaign matches
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Interspersed between tournament matches; only ruby and diamond accessible in 2-player mode

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Mortal Kombat SNES technical documentation)
wikipedia (Mortal Kombat 1992 video game - gameplay mechanics, campaign structure, narrative context)
